I've helped dozens of people get started on content platforms over the past two years (I work on the management/operations side). Here's the honest step-by-step:

**Step 1: Choose your platform wisely**

If you have zero following on social media, start with a live streaming platform — Chaturbate or Stripchat. They provide free traffic. You go live, viewers show up.

Don't start with OnlyFans if you have no audience. OF gives you zero visibility. Every subscriber must come from your social media. Without 1,000+ followers on Twitter/Instagram/TikTok, you'll sit there with 2 subscribers wondering what went wrong.

**Step 2: Get basic equipment ($100-150)**

You don't need thousands of dollars. Here's the minimum:

- Webcam: Logitech C920 or C922 ($50-70). Don't use your laptop camera — the quality difference is night and day.
- Light: 10" ring light ($25). This matters more than the camera. Good lighting makes a cheap camera look decent. Bad lighting makes an expensive camera look terrible.
- Microphone: Any USB condenser mic ($25). Fifine K669B works fine. Your viewers will forgive grainy video but they won't forgive echo and background noise.

That's it for equipment. Everything else (green screens, fancy backdrops, multiple lights) can come later when you're earning.

A more detailed equipment breakdown with options at different price points: [equipment guide](https://journal-bice-seven.vercel.app/equipment).

**Step 3: Set up your digital safety BEFORE going live**

This is the step most beginners skip. Don't.

- Create a separate email (ProtonMail) for all work accounts
- Get a virtual phone number — don't use your real one
- Install a VPN and keep it on
- Learn to strip EXIF data from photos (GPS coordinates can be embedded in images)
- Use a password manager with 2FA on everything

One evening of setup prevents months of problems.

**Step 4: Register and verify**

Sign up on your chosen platform. You'll need:
- Government ID (for age verification — this is required on all legitimate platforms)
- Bank account or payment processor (Paxum is popular internationally)

Verification takes 1-3 days. Use this time to set up OBS Studio (free streaming software) and do test streams to yourself.

**Step 5: Set realistic expectations**

First month: $200-600 if you stream consistently (4+ hours/day, 4-5 days/week). Some people make less. Few make more. This is normal.

Third month: $500-1,500 if you've been consistent and learned what works for your audience.

Six months: $1,000-3,000 if you've built a following and added supplementary income (OnlyFans/Fansly alongside streaming).

If someone promises you $5,000+ from month one — they're selling you something.

**Step 6: Treat it like a job**

Set a schedule and stick to it. Stream at the same time every day. Platforms reward consistency with better visibility. Your viewers will learn when you're online and come back.

The people who quit after two weeks because "it didn't work" are the same people who streamed at random times with no schedule and wondered why nobody showed up.

**What to avoid:**
- Studios that charge you upfront for "training"
- Exclusive contracts (especially early on)
- Anyone who pressures you to do things you're uncomfortable with
- Comparing yourself to top earners — they have years of experience and usually a team

For a comprehensive beginner's guide covering all of this in detail: [starter guide](https://journal-bice-seven.vercel.app/start-guide).

Feel free to ask follow-up questions — happy to help with specifics.
